mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or trigger-based Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high percentiles of RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which increases corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion regimes, or out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds 0.4%, or trigger-based alpha is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative or zero alpha over 20-day horizons, or joint high-growth periods produce negative SPX returns in greater than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of instances where INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or interaction IC remains below 0.02.
